title: Support for functionality similar to docker load
body:
Hello,

I was trying to search for another issue similar to what I have, the closest one was described in this one:
Does skopeo supports loading local docker tar.gz file into remote registry? #2247 

Basically what I get from that issue is that skopeo does not have plans to support a functionality to load multiple images from a tar.gz image. Is that statement correct? Or are there any plans or would it be possible to contribute to the project adding this functionality?

For example if I run the following command I get:
```
skopeo copy docker-archive:<some tar.gz with multiple images> dir:<some target dir>
FATA[0004] Error determining manifest MIME type for docker-archive:<some tar.gz>: Unexpected tar manifest.json: expected 1 item, got 8
```

For example for a manifest file like the one below:
```
[
    {
        "Config": "045ce52ecd52c74ebd7e53391f37fc9110e87aa1fe38ee00d769aac2b26ac338.json",
        "RepoTags": [
            "blabla/test:1"
        ],
        "Layers": [
            "c4b82aaec5c7ef585fc565122990b0a3e8d56d4d312676c0abe44a522aa5ff89/layer.tar",
            "82512aba5ef21b396bcd5509b97f5880fcd91d9877e669b54c78d9146aa8a4bf/layer.tar"
        ]
    },
    {
        "Config": "9d8f4a6aac64ce3d7ea861f4064ab0ca0d7cea079f81a4bac5ef7d5369ab71bd.json",
        "RepoTags": [
            "blabla/test2:1"
        ],
        "Layers": [
            "fb41d6fac32f95aab3750a9577c7a3830a3053f4cfc55c9196e850cb75def08d/layer.tar",
            "d2be7a24b6cdaa7dae6045416243221989610add1588361ca512f8080db075ea/layer.tar"
        ]
    }
]
```

My ultimate goal is not to load something into a directory but it is just an example, the expected result should be able to load all images to the registry. Similar to what the docker load or podman load command can do with the tarball with a single command.

Currently I need to manually iterate over the tags and run skopeo per each image and tag, for small tarballs that is not a big issue, but once the tarball gets bigger, I believe skopeo needs to do tarball extraction every time and that is what probably makes it very slow.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

We generally discourage using the `docker save` format — the way accessing multiple images is inefficient is one of the reasons; that it represent the full original representation of the images is another.

It should be fairy easy to write a local Go program using the underlying https://github.com/containers/image , which does this efficiently (only extracting the archive once). There is no obvious place for that functionality in Skopeo — `skopeo sync` would be the closest one, but it would need restructuring.

Typically, my recommendation for site-to-site copies is to run a temporary registry in a container, with the storage backed by a volume, and to transfer the backing volume to the other site.

OK I see, thanks for the tip, too bad that it is not supported, I do not have control over the tarballs, so I end up using podman load, and then podman push for the individual tags. It is still faster than doing it with skopeo, as the load happens only once.

Thanks!
